course_objectives: To provide knowledge on digital signal processing techniques and to design and implement IIR and FIR digital filters.

topics.topic: 1.1 Basic elements of Digital Signal Processing
topics.note: Explicitly asked in 2024Sp, 2021F, 2020F, 2018F, and 2017Sp (short note). Block diagram explanation is the most common form.

topics.topic: 1.2 Energy signal, Power signal
topics.note: Explicitly asked in 2021Sp Q1a and 2018F Q7c (short note).

topics.topic: 1.3 Need of Digital Signal Processing over Analog Signal Processing
topics.note: Advantages of DSP over ASP explicitly asked in 2024Sp, 2021F, 2020F, 2019Sp, 2016F and as part of broader Q1 in several others.

topics.topic: 1.4 Sampling continuous signals and spectral properties of sampled signals
topics.note: Nyquist rate, aliasing, sampling frequency questions appear in 2023Sp, 2022F, 2020F, 2019F, 2019Sp, 2017F, 2017Sp, 2015Sp. Most frequently as a calculation-based Q1a.

topics.topic: 2.1 Elementary discrete-time signals
topics.note: Explicitly asked to define/discuss in 2024F Q1a and 2020F Q1b.

topics.topic: 2.2 Discrete time Fourier series and properties
topics.note: DTFS properties (time shifting, time reversal) explicitly asked in 2020F Q1b and 2018F Q2a.

topics.topic: 2.3 Discrete time Fourier transform and properties
topics.note: DTFT properties (convolution, time shifting) explicitly asked in 2020F Q1b, 2018F Q2a, and 2017Sp Q2a (Parseval's theorem).

topics.topic: 2.4 Discrete time system properties (causality, stability, linearity, time-invariance)
topics.note: One of the most frequently tested topics. Classifying systems and proving properties appears in nearly all papers. Papers without: 2021F, 2020F, 2018F, 2017F, 2015Sp have indirect coverage.

topics.topic: 2.5 Properties of Linear Time-Invariant systems (LTI)
topics.note: LTI properties beyond causality/stability (superposition, shift-invariance proof) explicitly asked in 2021Sp Q1b, 2017F Q2b, 2016F Q1b, 2015Sp Q1b.

topics.topic: 2.6 LTI convolution sum characterized by constant coefficient difference equations
topics.note: MOST FREQUENTLY TESTED TOPIC. Linear convolution calculation appears in virtually every paper. Papers include: 2024F, 2024Sp, 2023Sp, 2022F, 2021Sp, 2021F, 2020F, 2019Sp, 2019F, 2018F, 2017F, 2016F, 2015Sp.

topics.topic: 2.7 Stability of LTI systems, Implementation of LTI system
topics.note: BIBO stability conditions explicitly asked in 2022F, 2021Sp, 2020F (short note), 2019F, 2023Sp (short note), 2017Sp.

topics.topic: 2.8 Frequency Response of LTI systems
topics.note: Frequency/magnitude response explicitly asked in 2024F (short note), 2018F (short note), 2017Sp Q2b.

topics.topic: 3.1 Definition of z-transform
topics.note: Z-transform definition and calculation appears in almost every paper. Nearly universal question.

topics.topic: 3.2 Convergence of Z-transform, Region of convergence (ROC)
topics.note: ROC plot, definition, and properties explicitly asked in 2023Sp, 2022F, 2021Sp, 2021F, 2020F, 2019Sp, 2018F, 2017F (OR), 2017Sp, 2016F (OR), 2015Sp.

topics.topic: 3.3 Properties of Z-Transform (linearity, time shift, convolution, differentiation, time reversal, multiplication)
topics.note: Z-transform properties explicitly asked in 2024F (short note), 2018F Q2b (ROC properties), 2017Sp Q2a (convolution and Parseval's), and 2020F Q2b.

topics.topic: 3.4 Inverse z-transform - by long division, by partial fraction expansion
topics.note: Inverse Z-transform asked in 2024Sp (log function), 2022F (log function), 2019F Q3a, 2017F Q3b, 2016F Q3a (multiple ROC), 2015Sp Q3a (multiple ROC).

topics.topic: 4.1 Definition and application of DFT
topics.note: DFT definition and why DFT is needed over DTFT asked in 2023Sp, 2022F, 2021Sp, 2020F, 2019F, 2017Sp, 2016F.

topics.topic: 4.2 Frequency response of LTI system via DFT
topics.note: Relationship between DFT and Z-transform / frequency response explicitly asked in 2015Sp Q3b and 2019Sp Q4b.

topics.topic: 4.3 Forward and Reverse DFT transform
topics.note: DFT/IDFT computation questions appear in nearly all papers. Covered implicitly in almost every circular convolution and FFT question.

topics.topic: 4.4 Properties of DFT (linearity, symmetry, time shift, frequency shift, duality, circular convolution, multiplication)
topics.note: Circular convolution of two sequences is one of the most frequently tested DFT problems. Appears in 2024F, 2024Sp, 2023Sp, 2022F, 2021Sp, 2021F, 2020F, 2019Sp, 2019F, 2018F, 2017F.

topics.topic: 4.5 Basic concept of Fast Fourier Transform (FFT) algorithm
topics.note: MOST FREQUENTLY TESTED IN CHAPTER 4. DIT-FFT or DIF-FFT 8-point computation asked in 2024F, 2024Sp, 2022F, 2021Sp, 2021F, 2020F, 2019F, 2018F, 2017F, 2017Sp, 2016F, 2015Sp.

topics.topic: 5.1 FIR filter overview
topics.note: FIR vs IIR comparison and overview explicitly asked in 2024Sp, 2023Sp, 2020F, 2019F (short note), 2018F.

topics.topic: 5.2 Structures for FIR Filter (direct, cascade, frequency sampling, lattice)
topics.note: FIR lattice structure/coefficient computation asked in 2024F Q3b, 2021Sp Q4b, 2020F Q4b, 2019Sp Q5a, 2017Sp Q4a.

topics.topic: 5.3 IIR Filter overview
topics.note: IIR filter definition and overview (beyond comparison with FIR) asked in 2018F Q5a, 2016F Q4a (DFT-multiplication relationship), and 2019F Q4b.

topics.topic: 5.4 Structure for IIR filter (direct form I & II, cascade, lattice, lattice ladder)
topics.note: MOST HEAVILY TESTED TOPIC IN CHAPTER 5. At least one IIR realization question in every paper. Includes direct form I/II, cascade, parallel, and lattice/lattice-ladder.

topics.topic: 5.5 Quantization of filter coefficients and effects on poles and zeros
topics.note: Explicitly asked only in 2017Sp Q7c as a short note.

topics.topic: 6.1 Gibbs phenomena in FIR filter design
topics.note: Asked as short note in 2022F Q7a, 2021Sp Q7c, 2017F Q7a.

topics.topic: 6.2 Filter Design by Window method (rectangular, hanning, hamming)
topics.note: Window-based FIR design (Hanning, Hamming, rectangular) asked in 2024F, 2024Sp, 2023Sp, 2021F, 2020F, 2019Sp, 2018F, 2017F, 2017Sp.

topics.topic: 6.3 Filter design by Kaiser window
topics.note: Kaiser window design or Kaiser window as short note appears in 2024F, 2024Sp, 2023Sp, 2021Sp, 2021F, 2020F, 2019Sp, 2019F, 2018F. Exact same specification repeats across many papers.

topics.topic: 6.4 Filter design by frequency sampling
topics.note: Frequency sampling design method asked in 2023Sp (short note) and 2017Sp Q6a.

topics.topic: 6.5 Filter design using the Remez exchange algorithm
topics.note: Remez exchange asked as short note in 2024Sp Q7c and 2016F Q7b.

topics.topic: 7.1 Filter design using low pass approximations - Butterworth filter
topics.note: Butterworth digital filter design (via BLT or IIM) explicitly asked in 2024F, 2021Sp, 2021F, 2020F, 2019F, 2017Sp, 2016F (via BLT), 2015Sp.

topics.topic: 7.2 Filter design using impulse invariance method
topics.note: IIR filter design via impulse invariance asked in 2024F, 2023Sp, 2022F, 2021Sp, 2021F, 2019Sp, 2018F (short note), 2015Sp.

topics.topic: 7.3 Filter design using bilinear transformation
topics.note: MOST FREQUENTLY TESTED IN CHAPTER 7. Bilinear transformation IIR design appears in 2024F, 2024Sp, 2023Sp, 2021Sp, 2021F, 2020F, 2019F, 2018F, 2017Sp, 2016F, 2015Sp.

topics.topic: 7.4 Properties of Chebyshev and Elliptic filters
topics.note: Chebyshev filter design explicitly asked only in 2022F Q6b.

topics.topic: 7.5 High pass, Band pass and Notch filters
topics.note: Not commonly explicitly asked as a standalone topic. Appears implicitly in general filter design questions. Direct reference seen in 2017F Q6b (matched-z for bandpass resonant filter).

references: A.V. Oppenheim, Discrete-Time Signal Processing, Prentice Hall, 1990
J.G. Proakis and D.G. Manolakis, Digital Signal Processing, Prentice Hall of India
S.K. Mitra, Digital Signal Processing, A Computer-based Approach, McGraw Hill

verification_note.range: 2015–2024 (Pokhara University BE, Digital Signal Analysis and Processing)
verification_note.methodology: Frequency = number of papers where the topic is DIRECTLY and EXPLICITLY asked as a main question or short note. Topics counted only once per paper even if asked multiple times.
high_frequency_topics: 2.6 LTI convolution sum (freq: 13) — appears in virtually every paper
5.4 IIR filter structures — direct form, cascade, lattice, lattice-ladder (freq: 13)
6.2 Window method FIR design (Hanning, Hamming, rectangular) (freq: 9)
